stream_samples.text: third meeting today where Zoom dropped me into 'reconnecting...' for 40 seconds on hotel wifi. teams call from the same network was fine.
AI Companion summary said our director 'committed to a Q3 launch' — he literally said the opposite. now I have to walk it back in writing.
[#MEET-9112 P1] customers on macOS Sonoma 14.5 report echo from their own mic ~30s into call, only when AirPods Pro 2 are connected
client metrics: median reconnect duration on lossy networks went from 4.2s to 11.6s after the 6.1.4 client rolled out. regression.
breakout rooms keep dumping me back to main session every time host re-broadcasts. lost 8 minutes of class redoing groups.
Zoom Phone caller ID showing 'Unknown' for half my inbound calls since the carrier change last month. ATA logs look fine.
[#VIDEO-7340 P2] screen share on Linux client (Ubuntu 22.04) drops to 4-6 fps when sharing Chrome with hardware accel on
Whiteboard updates lag 8-12 seconds for remote participants — looks like the websocket batch flush isn't keeping up on >20 person sessions
AI Companion attributed a quote to me that I never said. I wasn't even unmuted. how do I dispute this in the post-meeting recap?
support is escalating macOS echo cluster — 142 enterprise tickets this week, all Sonoma 14.5+, all AirPods/Beats. CSAT for the segment dropped 9pp.
Webinar attendees on flaky 4G can't rejoin after a drop without re-registering — the rejoin token expires in 60s
Zoom AI Companion saved me 20 min of post-meeting notes today. when it's right, it's a productivity unlock.
anyone else's Zoom Phone disconnecting after exactly 4 hours on a single call? happens daily, looks like a session timer
[meeting-sdk] reconnect logic on packet loss > 8% retries with same RTP port instead of NAT-rebind, blocking recovery on symmetric NATs
Enterprise admin: AI Companion summaries are blocked by our DLP policy but the toggle keeps flipping back on after every client update

clusters.title: Meeting reconnect failures on lossy networks
Breakout room re-entry drops on rebroadcast
Linux screen share frame rate collapse
Whiteboard sync lag on large sessions

top_epic.title: Harden meeting reconnect on lossy and roaming networks
top_epic.rationale: 2,480 signals across X, Zendesk and Slack converge on the same failure: clients sit in 'reconnecting...' for 8-12 seconds and frequently never recover on hotel/4G/symmetric-NAT networks since the 6.1.4 rollout. Median reconnect time regressed from 4.2s to 11.6s, and Webinar rejoin tokens expire before users can re-register. Competing platforms recover transparently on the same networks.
top_epic.verdict: Reliability-critical — every dropped reconnect is a visible product failure in front of customers.
top_epic.recommendation: Treat reconnect as a first-class SLO. Fix NAT-rebind on packet loss, extend rejoin token TTL, and ship a client-side recovery telemetry stream before any new AI surface area lands.
acceptance_criteria: Median reconnect time < 5s p95 on 8% packet-loss profile
NAT-rebind triggered on RTP timeout instead of port retry
Webinar rejoin token TTL extended to 5 min with rotating refresh
Client emits reconnect.recovered event with cause + duration

evidence.quote: Median reconnect duration on lossy networks went from 4.2s to 11.6s after 6.1.4.
Third meeting today where Zoom dropped me into 'reconnecting...' for 40 seconds.
Reconnect retries with same RTP port instead of NAT-rebind, blocking symmetric NATs.
